item.title: Kid has talent
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: The U.S. Congress should not get rich.
item.description: In fact, any public office should not have a money incentive. As we see, this draws the wrong type of people, the wrong type of governance, and really plays to the whole entire greed component of the human condition. This is why we are where we are. Too much money in politics, and too little attention paid by the American people until it was too late. Do you support taking money out of politics? http://stevens.house.gov/media/press-releases/rep-stevens-introduces-congressional-stock-trading-ban-additional-measures
item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered

item.description: Farah Bakaari got invited to an art show by her neighbor, artist Catharine O’Neill. She specifically asked her to wear a yellow dress. Although the request seemed unusual, Bakaari agreed and went to the show without knowing the reason behind it.
item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: Saturday sillies and memes
item.description:
Welcome to this dump of random memes. Sometimes I make them themed, sometimes I don't. If you enjoy laughing without talking politics, getting away from the horrors of the world, or escaping reality, you’ll love following this page!
Mmm man legs
Jesus, I can explain. It was funny.
Can I join this biker gang?
It was much easier to see then.
No, she has urukai hair
OMG kids are so mean
My fursona? It’s a naked mole rat. There is no fur.
My kid has a speech impediment, so it was worse when she was little. She couldn’t say ‘fart’ to save her life. It sounded like ‘fark’ in baby speak, so people thought she was saying ‘fuck’. Her great grandma, who hated cursing, thought it was hilarious and would ask her to say fart all the time.
I personally don’t have that much patience
Yeah, Elsa! What gives?
Did laugh out loud
Eating healthy isn’t that hard
Blasphemy but I think Jesus would laugh
Don’t laugh. Meemaw got pregnant 14 times in this outfit.
Okay, don’t give up on bees but this is a funny story
Me with my husband. Only he is okay to talk to me.
As a parent, I also don’t want to sit by my crying baby. However, I also want to cry so I get why my baby is crying.
lol
Him so cute!!!
My old lady cat does this
It’s because the rest of the world are freaks
My soul think my art is crappy
Send to all your friends
$$$
That first story? Happens to me all the time
I hate pez but, as a kid, it was sugar so I ate it
Definitely need
Genius
Please share yours!
Just draw what my brain sees!
Mmm grimy men on TV are great. In real life, stinky.
Most men are afraid of being fabulous
Men in the 80s thought this was fine but now it’s ‘gay’
And it’s food and clothing.
I love going to see my mom because someone cooks homemade food that I didn’t make
My child told me that she thought I was 58. I’m 39.
Every time
Petty
Yep
lol
Oops. Didn’t her kids remember?
What fresh hell is this?
We’re all still kids inside
Truth hurts
Have a coffee on me
I love this
item.dc:creator: GigglySprinkleCupcake

item.title: 100% transparency
item.description:
I’m not usually this honest with you all, but here we go. I know I’ve spent the last two weeks being all excited for mama Bobbie to give birth, and that I took a week off of work to be here for it, and I skipped my trip to Guatemala, but it was not a thing where I want to cuddle with cute little newborns and get cute pics and videos. I’m just scared. I’m really, really scared. Mama Sky’s birth was super traumatic for me. It was five hours of pulling kittens out of her and ripping their sacks and clearing their mouths and rubbing their chests to get them breathing. And then keeping them warm and putting them with mama. And then five of the six kittens died anyways. This desire to be here with mama Bobbie during her birthing process is not because I want cute kittens to snuggle with. It’s because I want these kittens to stay ALIVE and I feel like if I’m here, they WILL stay alive.
Forgive my honesty. There’s no reason to think that mama Bobbie won’t be the perfect mom. And have a flawless birth. But I have major PTSD from the last black cat to give birth on my kitten room mattress. I have full faith in Bobbie. She survived a dog attack while pregnant like a BAD ASS and is still super sweet and affectionate. I love her so much and I know she will be a great mama. But any and all good thoughts and vibes you can send this way are much appreciated
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: There goes the neighborhood....
item.description: I came across a video showing how new camera and AI systems can track everyone who walks down a street for 24 hours and then “overlay” all those people at once. It makes it incredibly easy to spot unusual foot‑traffic patterns — including things like potential drug houses. That part is genuinely useful. But this same tech can also be misused in ways that are worth thinking about as a community. Here are some examples of how this kind of system could be abused if there aren’t strong limits: • Monitoring and profiling people – Tracking who attends political gatherings, protests, or community meetings – Identifying organizers or people who meet frequently, even if they’ve done nothing wrong – Targeting specific neighborhoods more heavily than others • Commercial or corporate misuse – Hyper‑targeted advertising based on where you walk – Insurance companies using your movements to adjust premiums – Retailers tracking your path through a neighborhood to predict your shopping habits – Companies monitoring competitors’ employees to infer business strategy • Personal misuse – Someone with access tracking an ex‑partner’s daily routine – Following journalists, activists, or whistleblowers – Using movement data for blackmail or harassment • Law‑enforcement overreach – “Fishing expeditions” where normal behavior gets flagged as suspicious – Misinterpreting innocent visits (caregivers, friends, contractors) as something nefarious – Using movement patterns as a pretext for stops or searches – Predictive‑policing loops where more surveillance creates more “suspicious activity” • Criminal or foreign misuse – Criminals tracking when people leave or return home – Mapping the routines of officials, military personnel, or business leaders And once systems like this exist, they tend to expand. They often start with “only for serious crimes,” then slowly broaden to property crimes, then traffic enforcement, then general monitoring — until they’re simply always on. History shows that surveillance tools rarely shrink; they grow. What would make this safer? Strong legal oversight (like warrants and independent audits), strict technical controls (like encrypted logs and role‑based access), and public transparency about where and how these systems are used. Sunlight doesn’t solve everything, but it makes misuse harder to hide. Bottom line: This technology can absolutely help with real problems — but it also deserves real conversation about privacy, oversight, and how it should and shouldn’t be used. Staying informed helps ensure safety tools don’t quietly become something else. If anyone has thoughts or has seen similar systems, I’d be interested in hearing your perspective. from https://www.reddit.com/r/ObscurePatentDangers/comments/1rzdqbo/no_matter_where_you_go_whether_its_the_bustling/
item.dc:creator: RichardPenne

item.title: The Only President In 130 Years Without A Dog. Coincidence Unlikely. The Eagles Could Not Be Reached For Comment.
item.description: Donald Trump is afraid of sharks, windmills, oceans, rain, germs, stairs, ramps, dogs, food poisoning, crowds and EAGLES. The national bird. HE IS AFRAID OF THE FUCKING NATIONAL BIRD. This man has nuclear launch codes and is currently running an active war in the Middle East. ??? Let's review: He held an umbrella at the bottom of Air Force One stairs and then abandoned it. He has repeatedly discussed sharks with genuine terror. He demanded the ramp at West Point be investigated. He requires McDonald's because he fears food poisoning. THE PRESIDENT FEARS RAMPS. ?☂️? The windmill fear is perhaps the most spectacular. He believes windmills cause cancer. He has discussed this at length. Multiple times. In public. To crowds. While being the Commander in Chief of the most powerful military in human history. WINDMILL. CANCER. HE STILL BELIEVES THIS. ?️?? Not afraid of anything said the man afraid of sharks, ramps, rain, dogs, stairs, eagles, windmill cancer, germs, oceans, crowds and food poisoning. He is currently managing a war, an oil crisis and an economy. The sharks are reportedly unbothered. ???? #TrumpFears #AfraidOfEagles #WindmillCancer #RampGate #NationalBird #SharkFear #NotAfraidOfAnything #CommanderInChief #NuclearCodes #TheRampWasInvestigated
item.dc:creator: JamieGeorge

item.title: GovBrief Today #414
item.description: Denmark watched the United States kick in the doors in Caracas and deployed its military ten days later to make sure Greenland wasn't next. That's what the world learned two days ago when Danish broadcaster DR published an investigation based on more than a dozen official sources. Copenhagen endured Trump's threats throughout 2025. He sent the vice president against their wishes in March and ran a covert influence operation all summer. After Thanksgiving he appointed the governor of Louisiana as a special envoy whose stated mission was "to make Greenland a part of the U.S." Everyone said it was bluster, Trump being Trump, until January 3, when American special forces captured the president of Venezuela in his own capital and flew him to a jail cell in Brooklyn. The deputy chief of staff's wife posted an American flag over Greenland with one word: "SOON." Denmark immediately deployed its military to defend Greenland. Soldiers carried explosives to destroy their own runways and blood from national hospitals to treat the wounded. Under a fake NATO exercise, France sent mountain infantry, special forces, a frigate, and tanker aircraft. Germany, Sweden, the Netherlands, and Norway sent their own soldiers. Any American attack would mean firing on five allied nations at once. Trump spent a year threatening to take Greenland and today called every one of those countries cowards on social media because they won't help him clean up the war he started in Iran. This should be the only story in America tonight and most people will never see it. Tell the people in your world who are understandably shying away from all the bad news these days. Make them understand that Europe was prepared to go to war with the United States sixty-seven days ago. We've emailed 6,600+ stories over 414 straight nights. The algorithm doesn't always show them to you. Subscribe now at govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Today on the Website: https://go.govbrief.today/260320-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/embassy-diesel [2] https://go.govbrief.today/petro-dea [3] https://go.govbrief.today/greenland-defense [4] https://go.govbrief.today/nato-cowards [5] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-mar20 [6] https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-access [7] https://go.govbrief.today/dc-guard-2029 [8] https://go.govbrief.today/palantir-pentagon [9] https://go.govbrief.today/taylor-police [10] https://go.govbrief.today/swalwell-pulte [11] https://go.govbrief.today/boat-attack-46 [12] https://go.govbrief.today/clergy-ice-minn [13] https://go.govbrief.today/zampolli-melania [14] https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-sued-again [15] https://go.govbrief.today/house-arlington-miller [16] https://go.govbrief.today/cta-sues [17] https://go.govbrief.today/army-navy-game [18] https://go.govbrief.today/nj-sues-ice Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos
